_comment: Dual-ESP32 Redundancy & Failover Configuration — Critical system redundancy with true sensor redundancy, relay selector, and hot-swap procedures
_safety_class: IEC 60945 Ed.4 awareness, ABYC A-33 compliance

dual_node_roles._comment: Only critical subsystems receive dual-ESP32 redundancy. Non-critical subsystems use single ESP32s.

redundant_roles.failure_consequence: Loss of heading hold — helmsman must steer manually

placement.rationale: Opposite sides of wheelhouse — localized flooding/fire cannot destroy both

redundant_roles.failure_consequence: Loss of engine alarms — over-temp or low oil pressure undetected

placement.rationale: Opposite sides of engine room — localized flooding cannot destroy both

non_redundant_roles.failure_consequence: Manual lighting control required at breaker panel
Loss of automatic monitoring, manual operation still possible
No PTZ or streaming, cameras may still work standalone
Wind data unavailable to autopilot wind mode
Manual light switching at breaker panel

failover._comment_detection: STANDBY monitors HB_IN line (GPIO17). ACTIVE pulls LOW for 10ms every 100ms. If HB_IN stays HIGH for >500ms → HEARTBEAT_LOST.
failover._comment_transition: Failover target is <100ms from heartbeat loss to first corrective output. Heading wobble estimated at 1-2 degrees, self-corrects in 1-2 seconds.

compass_thresholds.sensor_disagree_action: Log warning, continue operating, alert Jetson

rudder_thresholds.description: Rudder readings should be very consistent (mechanical coupling)

graceful_handover.trigger: STANDBY detects ACTIVE health score declining AND STANDBY's score is higher
graceful_handover.process: STANDBY requests handover → ACTIVE acknowledges → both switch roles simultaneously
graceful_handover.purpose: Prevent hard failover when degradation is gradual (e.g., slow over-temperature)

post_sequence._comment: Power-On Self-Test runs on BOTH ESP32s simultaneously. Total duration ~800ms.

tests.name: flash_crc32
tests.description: Flash CRC-32 verification (stored hash vs computed)

tests.name: watchdog_arm
tests.description: Arm and verify external WDT reset capability

tests.name: i2c_scan
tests.description: I2C bus scan 0x08-0x77 for expected devices

tests.name: as5048a_read
tests.description: Read rudder angle within mechanical limits (-45° to +45°)

rules.description: If one ESP32 score >100 and other <=100, higher-score unit becomes ACTIVE
If both scores >100 AND differ by >20, higher score becomes ACTIVE
If scores within 20 points, GPIO21 jumper determines: HIGH (3.3V) = PRIMARY, LOW (GND) = BACKUP

rules.4, description: If BOTH scores <=100, neither drives outputs. ALARM mode. Captain must intervene.

role_assertion.active_behavior: Pull GPIO18 HIGH → energize relay coil → close relay → connect outputs to solenoids → run PID → send heartbeat → green LED ON
role_assertion.standby_behavior: GPIO18 LOW → relay open → outputs disconnected → monitor silently → yellow LED slow blink (1Hz)

jetson_handshake.action: Jetson acknowledges, syncs command state (heading target, mode)

relay_selector._comment: Relay selector board physically disconnects STANDBY ESP32 outputs from solenoids. Only ACTIVE can drive.

relays.label: PRIMARY
relays.description: Closes when PRIMARY asserts ACTIVE (GPIO18 HIGH)

relays.label: BACKUP
relays.description: Closes when BACKUP asserts ACTIVE (GPIO18 HIGH)

interlock.description: Hardware prevents both relays from being energized simultaneously even if firmware malfunction
interlock.guarantee: NEVER both relays closed at same time

truth_table._comment: R = relay coil energized (closed), X = relay open (default)
truth_table.note: State [HIGH, HIGH] should be impossible due to hardware interlock

hot_swap.description: Captain's step-by-step procedure for replacing a failed ESP32 at sea with zero configuration required
hot_swap._comment_breakdown: Unplug 30s + plug in 30s + OTA transfer 120s + reboot + verify 30s ≈ 3.5 minutes

steps.action: Open waterproof spare parts kit, remove spare ESP32 board
Unplug failed ESP32's M12 connector from RS-422 bus
Plug spare ESP32 into M12 connector
Spare ESP32 boots → sends 'announce' on serial → Jetson receives announce → sees unknown MAC
Jetson detects serial port for auto-provision → auto-assigns role → sends provision_new with stored calibration data + needs_ota=true
steps._comment: Auto-provision eliminates need for touchscreen interaction. Port is pre-configured for the role.

steps.action: Spare ESP32 enters OTA mode → receives role-specific firmware binary from Jetson over serial at 115200 baud (~2 min for 1.5MB)
steps._comment: Chunked transfer: 4KB chunks with ACK/NACK, max 3 retries per chunk

steps.action: ESP32 verifies SHA-256 of received firmware → sets OTA boot partition → sends ota_complete → reboots into assigned role
Rebooted ESP32 sends announce again (now with correct node_id) → Jetson recognizes node → sends provision_existing with all saved settings
ESP32 applies settings, calls role_module.init(), enters CONNECTED state. Verify green LED ON on node box.
steps._comment: Calibration data restored from Jetson SQLite backup. No manual calibration needed.

steps.action: Verify system operates correctly: check heading, test rudder response, confirm alarms work

auto_provision_faster.description: Pre-configure serial port to auto-assign role for any unknown MAC. Reduces procedure to: unplug old, plug in new, wait.

failover_timing._comment: Detailed timing breakdown of the failover event

breakdown.note: Verify HB still missing after 20ms debounce
Load target from NVS, reset integral
Time from heartbeat loss to first corrective output
From last heartbeat to full control restored

failover_timing.optimization_note: The 500ms heartbeat timeout is deliberately conservative (prevents false failovers during Flash writes that block for ~100ms). In testing, 200ms is sufficient.

degradation_levels._comment: System degrades gracefully. Captain ALWAYS has steering at every level.

levels.name: FULL
levels.description: Full operation: all Jetsons, all sensors, vision, voice, GPS track, adaptive autopilot, remote monitoring

levels.name: ONE_JETSON_DOWN
levels.description: One Jetson failed. Two remaining handle redistributed services.

levels.name: TWO_JETSONS_DOWN
levels.description: Two Jetsons failed. Single survivor provides limited services.
levels.lost: Vision, voice, LLM, GPS track, depth ML, fish ID, radar

levels.name: ALL_JETSONS_DOWN
levels.description: All Jetsons offline. ESP32s operate in standalone mode.
Both ESP32s in a redundant pair have failed (extremely unlikely).
capabilities: Manual steering (mechanical fallback via spring-center solenoids)
levels.lost: Autopilot, engine alarms, bilge monitoring — ALL ESP32-controlled systems
levels.action: Captain steers manually. Replace ESP32 pair from spare kit.
